data.title: 'Rally: A Guide to Building Resilience' Debuts to Help Leaders Guide Teams Through Uncertainty
data.description:
NEW YORK CITY, NEW YORK / ACCESS Newswire / March 6, 2026 / As thousands of executives gather this month at events like Transform 2026 to rethink the future of work, leadership advisor Greg Morley says the next monumental challenge is resilience.

In Rally: A Guide to Building Resilience, former Disney and LVMH senior executive Greg Morley offers a practical blueprint for how leaders can stabilize themselves and mobilize others when pressure is high and outcomes are uncertain. Drawing on decades of global leadership experience and interviews with twelve leaders whose lives span genocide survival, entrepreneurial collapse, corporate reinvention, and caregiving under extreme stress, Morley reframes resilience as a leadership discipline that can be developed and taught.

"Leaders often believe their job is to eliminate uncertainty," Morley says. "It isn't. Their responsibility is to help people move forward when uncertainty is unavoidable. The way leaders regulate themselves-how they communicate, how they make decisions, how they carry pressure-becomes the environment everyone else works inside."

Building on the themes of Morley's first book, Bond, which explored belonging and connection at work, Rally tackles the next leadership challenge: how to mobilize people when the ground is shifting. The book offers research-based guidance for the moments leaders face most often but are rarely trained for, including:

  • stabilizing teams after shocks or bad news

  • telling difficult truths without amplifying fear

  • restoring purpose when morale collapses

  • designing support systems instead of playing hero

  • helping teams regain momentum after setbacks

Each chapter pairs behavioral science and organizational research with powerful human stories that illustrate resilience in action. The result is a leadership guide that is both practical and deeply human.

"Rally is a refreshingly honest take on leadership under pressure," says Susan MacKenty Brady, bestselling author of Arrive and Thrive and All the Difference. "Greg Morley moves beyond performative resilience to show what leaders are truly responsible for when uncertainty is high: self-management, clarity, and genuine human connection."

Former Disney executive Djuan Rivers adds that the book reflects the real responsibility leaders carry when others look to them for direction. "What Greg captures in Rally is the human side of leadership under pressure," Rivers says. "Through powerful stories of loss, reinvention, success, and failure, he reminds us that leadership is less about control and more about how you carry yourself when it counts."

The book is available at Amazon and other fine book retailers such as Blackwell's Bookshop, Walmart, Hudson Booksellers, Strand Books, Bookazine (Hong Kong), Booktopia (Australia), Waterstones (Europe), and Feltrinelli Education (Italy).

For speaking engagements, workshops and one-on-one coaching to develop your resilience, contact Greg: greg@gregmorley.com.

SOURCE: Greg Morley

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Wall St opens lower as Middle East turmoil, weak jobs report weigh
data.description:
Wall Street's main indexes opened lower on Friday as the conflict raging in the Middle East threatened to fuel inflation through higher energy costs and data showed the economy unexpectedly shed jobs in February.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 320.2 points, or 0.67%, at the open to 47,634.55. The S&P 500 fell 61.7 points, or 0.90%, at the open to 6,769.03​, while the Nasdaq Composite dropped 327.8 points, or 1.44%, to 22,421.172 at the opening bell.

data.title: Ferrovial Receives $700 Million Contract to Build Railway Tunnel in Poland
data.description:
By Nina Kienle

Ferrovial said it received a contract valued at around 2.58 billion zlotys ($700.5 million) from Poland to build two sections of the new Malopolskie region rail line.

The infrastructure group on Friday said that this would be the first joint tunneling project between its construction division and polish subsidiary Budimex.

The consortium will develop 17.5 kilometers of electrified double-track railway, as well as 1.9 kilometers of single-track railway and two parallel tunnels, it said.

The new line will improve regional mobility and strengthen connections from Krakow to the south of the country, Ferrovial said.

The project is expected to last 46 months, it added.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: Ashford Hospitality Trust, CFO Deric Eubanks Agree to Termination
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Ashford Hospitality Trust's chief financial officer, Deric Eubanks, is resigning at the end of the month, according to recent securities filings.

The company and Eubanks agreed that, effective March 31, Eubanks will terminate his employment with the Ashford and its affiliates, according to filings submitted to the Securities and Exchange Commission.

Eubanks is also the CFO of affiliate Braemar Hotels & Resorts, and his service as finance chief with that company will also end on the same termination date, Ashford said.

Ashford will pay Eubanks about $1.8 million over the course of the next year, the company said. The amount represents his base salary plus the average cash incentive bonus received for the time he serves until his resignation, Ashford said.

Eubanks also has $3.3 million in deferred cash grants that will continue to vest and paid as part of the original schedule, the company said.

Justin Coe, the company's current chief accounting officer and principal accounting officer, will serve as the principal financial officer after the termination date, Ashford said.

Eubanks will also receive $200,000 for part-time work until the end of June to see through and support some projects he was working on before resigning.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: GMTech 2025 10-K: $3.48M Revenue, $0.03 EPS
data.description:
GMTech reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $3.478 million and net income of $413,355, translating to basic and diluted net income per share of $0.03 on 12,000,000 weighted average shares; the revenue increase reflects the company's transition into smartphone trade in Asia which materially boosted top-line results. Gross profit was reported at $2.661 million with operating income of $508,402, and operating cash flow turned positive in 2025 despite investing outflows tied to scaling the new trade business. The results mark a reversal from the prior-year net loss of $(32,557) as the company pivoted from IT consulting to smartphone trading.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue, net: $3.478 million (FY ended Oct 31, 2025)
  • Gross profit: $2.661 million
  • Operating income: $508,402
  • Net income: $413,355 (compared with $(32,557) prior year)
  • Net income per share — Basic and diluted: $0.03 (based on weighted average 12,000,000 shares)

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth and business pivot: Revenues surged from $52.8 thousand in 2024 to $3.48 million in 2025 following the launch and scale-up of a smartphone trade business in Asia, representing a significant shift from the company's prior focus on IT consulting.
  • Channel shift: The company pivoted its operating model toward smartphone trading, changing distribution and sales channels to support the new trade activities.
  • Inventory and working capital: Inventories increased materially to support smartphone sales, accompanied by higher merchant receivables and deferred revenue as trade operations scaled.
  • Operational scale-up investments: The company increased notes receivable and invested in property and equipment to finance and support expanded trade activities.
  • Cash flow dynamics: Operating cash flow turned positive in 2025 on profitable operations while the company experienced investing outflows to support the new smartphone business.

Original SEC Filing: GMTech Inc. [ GMTH ] - 10-K - Mar. 06, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Pakistan parliament passes Virtual Assets Act formalizing crypto regulatory authority
data.description:
Pakistan’s parliament passed the Virtual Assets Act, 2026, converting the Pakistan Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (PVARA) into a permanent federal body with the power to license and supervise crypto service providers. 

The legislation, which takes effect upon publication in the official Gazette, introduces criminal penalties for unlicensed operations, including fines of up to PKR 50 million ($179,000) and imprisonment of up to five years. Unauthorized virtual asset offerings or promotional activities will receive a separate penalty of up to PKR 25 million ($89,000) and three years imprisonment.

The law also authorizes PVARA to establish special “virtual asset zones” designed to attract blockchain companies, though specific zones have not yet been designated.

PVARA was initially constituted through a presidential ordinance in July 2025. The new law replaces that temporary measure with a statutory framework, granting the authority powers to acquire property, enter contracts, and enforce compliance. PVARA is now responsible for issuing, suspending, and revoking licenses for Virtual Asset Service Providers, which include exchanges, custodians, and firms facilitating token issuance.

“The legislation also equips the Authority with powers to address money laundering, terrorist financing, and other illicit activities associated with virtual assets, bringing Pakistan’s regulatory approach in line with international standards,” PVARA wrote in an X post on Friday.

Licensing and market entry

PVARA first invited global crypto firms to apply for licenses in September 2025, targeting an estimated 40 million local users. The regulator stipulated that applicants must already be recognized by a major jurisdiction such as the U.S., the European Union, or Singapore. 

Firms must also meet minimum capital requirements and ensure their services comply with Sharia law under the guidance of a committee of Islamic finance scholars.

HTX in December 2025 became one of the first exchanges to receive a No Objection Certificate from PVARA, initiating the formal licensing process. Binance received a similar preliminary clearance that same month. 

Both clearances allow the exchanges to register with Pakistan’s Financial Monitoring Unit for anti-money laundering compliance while preparing full license applications, though they are not yet permitted to operate.

Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data. As of November 2023, Foresight Ventures is a majority investor of The Block. Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. The Block continues to operate independently to deliver objective, impactful, and timely information about the crypto industry. Here are our current financial disclosures.

© 2026 The Block. All Rights Reserved. This article is provided for informational purposes only. It is not offered or intended to be used as legal, tax, investment, financial, or other advice.

data.title: Gold edges up after US payrolls report, but heads for first weekly fall in five
data.description:
Key points:
  • Israel strikes Hezbollah-controlled suburbs of Beirut
  • US dollar set for its strongest weekly rise in over a year
  • Silver, platinum, palladium head for weekly losses

By Anmol Choubey

Gold edged up on Friday after softer U.S. payrolls data kept hopes for a Federal Reserve interest rate cut alive, though a stronger dollar capped gains and left the metal on track for its first weekly decline in five weeks.

Spot gold was up 0.4% at $5,095.78 per ounce as of 09:02 a.m. ET (1402 GMT), but was down 3.4% so far this week. U.S. gold futures for April delivery rose 0.5% at $5,105.10.

"An alarmingly weak payrolls report that saw heavy private sector job losses along with higher wages whispers stagflation; let's see if this is enough to help gold recover from what has been a disappointing week," said Tai Wong, an independent metals trader.

Data showed that nonfarm payrolls decreased by 92,000 jobs last month, compared with economists’ expectations for a 59,000 gain, while the unemployment rate rose to 4.4%.

On the geopolitical front, Israel pounded Beirut after ordering an unprecedented evacuation of the entire southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital, a major expansion of the war against Iran it began a week ago alongside the United States.

The U.S. dollar index was set for its strongest weekly rise in over a year as the escalating conflict in the Middle East drove demand for safe-haven assets.

That has made dollar-priced gold costlier for overseas buyers, helping push the metal down despite its own reputation as a haven from risk.

"You have algorithmic sellers calibrated to automatically sell when the dollar strengthens, part of the underperformance in precious metals this week," said Hugo Pascal, a precious metals trader at InProved.

Fed policymakers will meet on March 18, where they are widely expected to hold rates steady, with the first cut widely expected in July, as per the CME FedWatch tool.

Gold is often viewed as a long-term inflation hedge, but typically performs well in low interest-rate environments because it yields no income. The yellow metal is up more than 18% so far this year.

As the Iran conflict raged, crude prices were headed for their sharpest weekly gain since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine in February 2022, stoking renewed inflation fears.

Spot silver rose nearly 0.7% to $82.73 per ounce. Spot platinum fell 0.7% to $2,107.82, while palladium lost 0.3% to $1,625.25. All metals were headed for weekly losses.

data.title: Information Services Group Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Information Services Group Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 8 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 6 cents. The mean expectation of five analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 8 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 7 cents to 8 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 5.9% to $61.21 million from a year ago; analysts expected $61.27 million.

  • Information Services Group Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 5 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $2.62 million.

  • Information Services Group Inc shares had fallen by 17.6% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "strong buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the it services & consulting peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Information Services Group Inc is $7.13, about 33.2% above its last closing price of $4.76

data.title: HDFC ͏Sky F͏&O Heatmaps: Identifying sector rotation beyond price movement
data.description:
Capital moves rarely at ran͏dom in the market. ͏It gradually moves from one sector to another as sentiment, liquidity, and positioning are evolving. These shifts are often initiated in derivative ͏data before price charts clearly reflect them.

Traders who focus only on price movement might miss early signs of rotation. HDFC Sky F&O Heatmaps fill this gap by integrating Open Interest, volume and price information in a structured͏ visual layout, helping traders to monitor sector rotation with more clarity and confidence.

Understanding Sector Rotation in t͏he͏ F&O Segment

Sector͏ rotation refers to the relative shift in activity across ͏industry segments within the derivatives market. In the Futures and Options (F&O) segment, such shifts are reflected ͏in the form of changes͏ in price, open interest and traded volume.

Such changes can take place among index components or stock derivatives traded under different sectors. Sector-level monitoring derivative data offer a broad view that goes beyond the price movement of a single stock.

Structured analysis of this data assists in identifying where ͏activity concentration is shifting͏ within the F&O segment.

What Makes ͏HDFC ͏Sky F&O Heatmaps Different?͏

Unlike traditional market dashboards that focus͏ ͏mainly on price͏ change, HDFC Sky’s F&O heatmaps combine:͏

  • Open Interest (OI) buildup
  • Volume activity
  • Price movement
  • Position classification, such as long buildup or short buildup

The system updates at regular 15-minute intervals, allowing traders to track developing patterns throughout the trading session. Instead of scanning multiple data tables, traders can quickly see where interest builds and where it fades.

This approach shifts focus from “Which sector moved?” to “Where is fresh capital entering or exiting?”

Key Components That Help Identify Rotation

The following components help traders detect sector rotation by analysing derivatives positioning and participation patterns within the market:

1. 15-Minute OI Buildup Analysis

The heatmap refreshes every 15 minutes. This short interval offers a real-time snapshot of position activity. If traders notice consistent long buildup in a sector across multiple intervals, it often signals sustained interest.

On the other hand, repeated short buildup or long unwinding across intervals may indicate capital leaving that sector.

Because the data updates during market hours, traders can track shifts as they happen rather than reviewing them at the end of the day.

2. Visualising Smart Money Movement

The heatmap identifies:

  • Long buildup (rising price and rising OI)
  • Short buildup (falling price and rising OI)
  • Short covering (rising price and falling OI)
  • Long unwinding (falling price and falling OI)

This classification helps traders interpret the intent behind price movement.

For example, if the Nifty Bank index shows long buildup while another sector shows short buildup, it suggests that traders may prefer banking exposure over others. This comparison helps detect sector rotation earlier than price charts alone.

3. Sector-Specific Drill-Down

The “Trade with Heatmaps” feature allows users to click on a sector block and view the individual stocks driving that movement.

Suppose the IT sector appears strongly positive. By drilling down, traders can identify whether one heavyweight stock drives the move or whether multiple stocks participate. Broad participation often signals stronger rotation.

This structured drill-down supports more informed decision-making rather than relying on surface-level observation.

How to Identify Sector Rotation Using HDFC Sky Heatmaps

To identify sector rotation effectively using the heatmaps within HDFC Sky, traders should focus on the following signals and analytical steps:

1. Spot Overperforming and Underperforming Sectors

The colour-coded heatmap makes it easy to identify sectors with strong long buildup or heavy short buildup. Green shades generally indicate long interest, while red shades suggest short pressure.

If one sector consistently turns greener while another turns redder across timeframes, it often signals rotation in progress.

2. Monitor Changing Trends Across Timeframes

Sector rotation does not always happen in a single session. Sometimes, it develops over days or weeks.

A sector that appears weak on a 1-day timeframe but turns stronger on a 1-week or 1-month view may indicate early accumulation. Comparing timeframes helps confirm whether the shift reflects short-term noise or sustained capital movement.

3. Validate With Open Interest Patterns

Open Interest offers confirmation.

Rotation into a sector (bullish indication):

  • Rising prices with rising OI suggest fresh long positions.

Rotation out of a sector (bearish indication):

  • Falling prices with rising OI indicate short buildup.
  • Falling prices with falling OI indicate long unwinding.

When traders combine price and OI together, they gain a clearer understanding of whether the move carries conviction.

4. Analyse Sector Concentration

The “Block View” highlights capital concentration across sectors. If traders notice money shifting from high-weightage sectors, such as banking, into other sectors like auto or pharma, the heatmap presents this shift visually.

This representation helps traders understand broader market positioning rather than focusing on individual stock movements.

Supporting Tools Within the HDFC Sky F&O Dashboard

The F&O ecosystem within HDFC Sky also includes complementary features that strengthen rotation analysis.

1. Market Movers & Scanners

These tools highlight stocks with unusual volume or price action. When a sector appears active on the heatmap, traders can use scanners to identify leading stocks within that sector.

2. FII/DII Activity Tracker

Institutional flows often influence sector rotation. The FII/DII Activity Tracker helps align sector signals with broader institutional positioning. If foreign institutional investors show increased activity in a sector that also shows long buildup on the heatmap, it strengthens the rotation thesis.

By combining heatmaps, scanners, and institutional data, traders gain a multi-layered perspective.

Why Sector Rotation Matters for F&O Traders

Futures and options traders often benefit from identifying strong sectors rather than isolated stocks. Sector strength tends to support sustained price movement, while weak sectors may limit upside even in fundamentally strong stocks.

The heatmap approach reduces dependence on speculation. It brings structure to decision-making by showing where participation increases and where it declines.

Instead of reacting after a trend becomes obvious, traders can position themselves as rotation unfolds.

Conclusion

Sector rotation often begins beneath the surface of price charts. HDFC Sky F&O Heatmaps reveal these shifts by combining Open Interest, volume, and price data into a clear visual format. By tracking 15-minute buildup patterns, sector participation, and institutional activity, traders can identify where capital flows next. This structured insight supports more informed F&O strategies and helps traders stay aligned with evolving market sentiment.
